entry.title: Resume
entry.summary:
<style>
    .resume { max-width: 900px; margin: 0 auto; }
    .resume h1 { margin-bottom: 0.2rem; }
    .resume .meta { margin-top: 0; line-height: 1.4; }
    .resume .meta a { text-decoration: none; }
    .resume hr { margin: 1rem 0; }
    .resume h2 { margin-top: 1.2rem; margin-bottom: 0.4rem; font-size: 1.25rem; }
    .resume .two-col { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; table-layout: fixed; }
    .resume .two-col td …</style>
entry.content:
Christine Hodgins

Winnipeg, MB, Canada
Mobile: +1 (431) 235 4893
Email: hodginsc@jeff.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/christinehodgins

Personal Profile

Robotics researcher specializing in Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models and robot learning for complex manipulation. I build end-to-end systems across simulation and hardware, with expertise in teleoperation, 3D vision, and collision-aware motion planning.

Education

2024 – 2026
MSc in Robotics
University of Manitoba, Winnipeg, MB
Full academic scholarship
Supervised by: John Doe
GPA: 3.88/4.00
Relevant Coursework: Visual Object Recognition, Robotic Intelligence, Autonomous Navigation
Thesis: Modular Primitives for Multi-Modal Robotic Action Models (In Progress)
2019 – 2023
B.Eng in Electrical and Electronic Engineering
University of Manitoba, Winnipeg, MB
GPA: 3.92/4.00
Honours: Dean's List, First Class Honours
Relevant Coursework: Linear Control Systems, Applied AI, Digital Signal Processing
Thesis: Adaptive Control for Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Systems
Key Research Interests: Robot Learning, Geometric Computer Vision, Teleoperation

Experience

May 2025 – Aug 2025
Robotics Research Intern - Tech Company or Research Lab
  • Engineered a VR-integrated teleoperation suite for industrial manipulators to facilitate large-scale VLA data collection.
  • Benchmarked deployment performance of state-of-the-art foundation models on physical hardware.
  • Investigated novel techniques for in-context policy adaptation in unstructured environments.
Sept 2023 – May 2024
Software Engineering Intern — Tech Company, Location
  • Programmed C# and C++ middleware for automated hardware validation systems.
  • Optimized legacy GUI modules, resulting in improved system response times during testing.
  • Collaborated with the systems team to integrate firmware updates for semiconductor equipment

Academic Publications

  • Geometry-Aware VLA Architecture: Infusing 3D Context into Vision-Language-Action Models.
    Under review. [arXiv]
    Christine Hodgins, Lance Sweets, Temperance Brennan
  • Self-Supervised World Modeling for Robotic Skill Acquisition.
    Under review. [arXiv]
    Angela Montenegro, Christine Hodgins, Seeley Booth
  • Hybrid Neural Networks for Robust Speech Emotion Classification.
    International Conference on ML.
    Christine Hodgins, Jack Hodgins, Thomas Edison

Projects / Research

2025
Autonomous Agricultural Manipulation System - University Robotics Lab
  • Developed a point-cloud-based pipeline for object identification and harvesting in occluded scenarios
  • Implemented collision-aware motion planning, reducing accidental contact with obstacles by over 65%.
  • Integrated the system using NVIDIA Isaac Sim and successfully validated on a physical robotic arm.

Skills

Python, C/C++, Linux (Ubuntu), ROS/ROS2, PyTorch, NVIDIA Isaac Sim, MATLAB
Languages: English (Fluent), French (Native)

References

Camilla Sorayan
Principal Investigator, Department of Robotics
University of Manitoba
Email: camillas@umanitoba.com

Daisy Sweets
Senior Research Scientist, Computer Vision Group
University of Manitoba
Email: daisys@umanitoba.com
